text: When working with APIs, you often need to send HTTP requests to a server and receive data back. Most modern APIs return this data in JSON format, which is easy to read and process in many programming languages. However, you might need authentication, like an API key, to access certain resources. It's also important to be aware of rate limits, which prevent you from making too many requests in a short period. If you ignore these limits, you may receive an error message. Good error handling means checking the server's response for problems and adjusting your code accordingly.
